channel.title: NYT > U.S. News
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Mixed Results for AIPAC and Allies in Illinois House Primaries
item.description: Organizations linked to the pro-Israel lobbying organization had some victories and some less-than-ideal outcomes in closely watched Democratic contests.
item.dc:creator: Kellen Browning
item.media:credit: Jim Vondruska for The New York Times
item.media:description: The American Israel Public Affairs Committee took credit on social media for its political maneuvering, suggesting it that contributed to Kat Abughazaleh’s loss.

item.title: Illinois Primary Election Takeaways: Stratton Wins and AIPAC’s Power Is Tested
item.description: Democratic voters put Lt. Gov. Juliana Stratton on a path to the Senate, while the pro-Israel lobby notched its first major victories of the year but also faced a tough defeat.
item.dc:creator: Shane Goldmacher
item.media:credit: Joshua Lott for The New York Times
item.media:description: Lt. Gov. Juliana Stratton of Illinois won the state’s Democratic primary race for Senate, backed by millions from Gov. JB Pritzker.

item.title: La Shawn K. Ford Wins Nomination for House Seat Long Held by Danny Davis
item.description: Mr. Ford, who received Mr. Davis’s endorsement, emerged from a wide field of candidates seeking to represent a district that includes the West Side of Chicago.
item.dc:creator: Mitch Smith
item.media:credit: Erin Hooley/Associated Press
item.media:description: La Shawn K. Ford, a state lawmaker, won the Democratic Party’s nomination.

item.title: Juliana Stratton Wins Illinois Democratic Senate Primary
item.description: Ms. Stratton, the state’s lieutenant governor, prevailed with millions of dollars of help from Gov. JB Pritzker, a billionaire. She will be heavily favored in the general election.
item.dc:creator: Reid J. Epstein
item.media:credit: Joshua Lott for The New York Times
item.media:description: Lt. Gov. Juliana Stratton will be on the ballot for Senate in Illinois in November.

item.title: Daniel Biss, Mayor of Evanston, Ill., Wins Democratic Nomination for House Seat
item.description: Mr. Biss emerged after being an early front-runner in the turbulent, crowded contest to replace a longtime incumbent in Illinois’s Ninth District.
item.dc:creator: Julie Bosman
item.media:credit: Joshua Lott for The New York Times
item.media:description: Mayor Daniel Biss of Evanston, Ill., at Fox Chicago’s debate last month.

item.title: Centrist Melissa Bean Wins 8th District Democratic Primary
item.description: Ms. Bean, a moderate former congresswoman, defeated a left-wing rival in the primary race for the Chicago-area seat to be vacated by Representative Raja Krishnamoorthi.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk
item.media:credit: Evan Vucci/Associated Press

item.title: Toni Preckwinkle, Longtime Chicago Democrat, Holds Off Moderate Challenger
item.description: Ms. Preckwinkle has been president of the Cook County Board for four terms. Brendan Reilly, a member of the Chicago City Council, mounted a challenge to her right in a Democratic primary.
item.dc:creator: Mitch Smith
item.media:credit: Joshua Lott for The New York Times
item.media:description: Toni Preckwinkle, president of the Cook County Board of Commissioners, is a four-term incumbent.

item.title: Don Tracy Wins Republican Senate Primary in Illinois
item.description: The former state party chair will begin the general election as a significant underdog in a heavily Democratic state.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk
item.media:credit: Dominic Di Palermo/Chicago Tribune, via Getty Images
item.media:description: Don Tracy, the former chair for the Illinois Republican Party, won the Republican primary for the open Senate seat in Illinois on Tuesday.

item.title: Darren Bailey Wins Second Chance to Challenge Illinois Gov. JB Pritzker
item.description: Mr. Bailey, a former lawmaker aligned with President Trump, lost to Mr. Pritzker in 2022. Mr. Pritzker had no opposition in a Democratic primary.
item.dc:creator: Mitch Smith
item.media:credit: E. Jason Wambsgans/Chicago Tribune, via Getty Images
item.media:description: Darren Bailey, a former state lawmaker, is running for governor of Illinois.

item.title: Donna Miller Beats Jesse Jackson Jr. in Illinois House Primary
item.description: Ms. Miller, a Cook County commissioner and a former health care consultant, blocked a comeback attempt from Mr. Jackson, who had served time in federal prison.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk
item.media:credit: Erin Hooley/Associated Press
item.media:description: Donna Miller cast herself as the candidate of the people.

item.title: U.S.P.S. Postmaster Says Service Will Be ‘Out of Cash’ in Under a Year
item.description: The U.S.P.S. leader said at a hearing that the service could run out of cash within a year and asked lawmakers to increase its borrowing limits.
item.dc:creator: Adam Sella
item.media:credit: Cliff Owen/Associated Press
item.media:description: Postmaster General David Steiner

item.title: Judge Orders Voice of America to Restart All News Operations
item.description: More than 1,000 full-time journalists and support staff at the news group were ordered to return to work by March 23 and to resume broadcasting.
item.dc:creator: Minho Kim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Agency for Global Media
item.media:credit: Jason Andrew for The New York Times
item.media:description: A federal judge’s ruling orders more than 1,000 full-time workers and support staff to return to work at Voice of America by March 23.

item.title: Police Kill Gunman Who Shot Employee at Georgia V.A. Clinic
item.description: The employee’s condition was not immediately disclosed. The Department of Veterans Affairs said the clinic would be closed for the rest of the week, while the authorities investigate.
item.dc:creator: Rylee Kirk
item.media:credit: Emilie Megnien/Associated Press
item.media:description: A gunman was fatally shot after he opened fire inside the Pickens County V.A. Clinic in Jasper, Ga., on Tuesday, wounding a worker there, the authorities said.

item.title: Year After Trump Takeover, Institute of Peace Is Little More Than Scenery
item.description: As a lawsuit by the former staff drags on, the fight highlights President Trump’s upending of traditional peace-building and the lasting effects of his administration’s cost-cutting blitz.
item.dc:creator: Aishvarya Kavi
item.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
item.media:description: The U.S. Institute of Peace was recently renamed the Donald J. Trump United States Institute of Peace.

item.title: Counties to Watch in the Illinois Democratic Senate Primary
item.description: There is more to the state than Chicago and its suburbs, even if that area makes up a large majority of the vote.
item.dc:creator: Christine Zhang
item.media:credit: Christine Zhang/The New York Times

item.title: In His Resignation Letter, Joe Kent Spoke About the Death of His Wife
item.description: Chief Petty Officer Shannon Kent was killed in action during a special operations mission in Syria in 2019. Mr. Kent said he could not support “sending the next generation off to fight and die” in Iran.
item.dc:creator: John Ismay
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Jenny Kane/Associated Press
item.media:description: Joe Kent, the director of the National Counterterrorism Center, resigned on Tuesday.

item.title: No Trump Endorsement for Cornyn or Paxton in Texas Senate Race as Deadline Passes
item.description: President Trump’s decision not to weigh in before the deadline means both John Cornyn and Ken Paxton remain on the ballot, extending their costly and increasingly personal battle into a May runoff.
item.dc:creator: Lauren McGaughy
item.media:credit: Al Drago, Tamir Kalifa for The New York Times

item.title: Trump’s Next Decision in War: Whether to Retrieve Iran’s Nuclear Fuel
item.description: A mission to seize or destroy Iran’s nuclear material would be one of the riskiest military operations in modern American history.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Vantor, via Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: A satellite image of the Pickaxe Mountain tunnel complex near the Natanz nuclear facility in central Iran last week. It is not certain where all of Iran’s nuclear material resides.

item.title: 5 Things to Know About Raja Krishnamoorthi, Democratic Senate Hopeful in Illinois
item.description: The congressman, an Ivy League-educated lawyer known for his fund-raising prowess, is aiming to become the second Indian American elected to the Senate.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk
item.media:credit: Joshua Lott for The New York Times
item.media:description: Across five terms in Congress, Representative Raja Krishnamoorthi has earned a reputation as an ambitious political operator.

item.title: 5 Things to Know About Juliana Stratton, Democratic Senate nominee in Illinois
item.description: The lieutenant governor of Illinois, who won the Democratic Senate nomination, is a close ally of Gov. JB Pritzker. She has said ICE should be abolished.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk
item.media:credit: Joshua Lott for The New York Times
item.media:description: Lt. Gov. Juliana Stratton of Illinois speaking to residents at Montclair Senior Residences of Calumet Heights in Chicago last week.

item.title: 5 Things to Know About Robin Kelly, Democratic Senate Hopeful in Illinois
item.description: The Senate hopeful has clashed with Gov. JB Pritzker, a fellow Democrat, and has the endorsement of the Congressional Black Caucus.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk
item.media:credit: Joshua Lott for The New York Times
item.media:description: Representative Robin Kelly of Illinois at a campaign event last week in Chicago.

item.title: Independent Polls are Scarce in the Illinois Democratic Senate Primary
item.description: In a rare open race for one of the state’s Senate seats, surveys suggest a wide range of potential outcomes.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Soler
item.media:credit: Nam Y. Huh/Associated Press
item.media:description: Representative Robin Kelly, Representative Raja Krishnamoorthi and Lt. Gov. Julianna Stratton at a primary debate in Chicago last month.

item.title: Kharg Island Is an Appealing Target for Trump, With High Risks
item.description: A U.S. attack or a move to seize control of Iran’s main oil export hub could cripple the country’s ability to profit from its natural resources. But it would also risk sending energy prices even higher.
item.dc:creator: Anton Troianovski, Rebecca F. Elliott and Peter Eavis
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Atta Kenare/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: The Kharg Island oil facility off the coast of Iran in 2017.

item.title: Trump Administration Offers Narrow Immigration Changes to End D.H.S. Shutdown
item.description: Administration officials have put forth several proposals to answer Democratic demands for major changes in President Trump’s crackdown in exchange for reopening the Department of Homeland Security.
item.dc:creator: Michael Gold
item.media:credit: Jamie Kelter Davis for The New York Times
item.media:description: Border Patrol agents in Minneapolis earlier this year. The White House’s letter did not address barring federal immigration officers from wearing masks, something that Democrats have demanded.

item.title: Jesse Jackson Jr. Seeks Comeback After Corruption Scandal
item.description: After his promising career was halted by a corruption scandal that sent him to prison, the former Democratic congressman is trying to return to the House.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk
item.media:credit: Jamie Kelter Davis for The New York Times
item.media:description: Former Representative Jesse L. Jackson Jr., who is running to reclaim his old district, has promised to fight for lower prices.

item.title: Miami Cubans Fear Trump Won’t Go Far Enough: ‘The Castros Have to Go’
item.description: Many Cubans in Florida have sought regime change for decades. They fear that President Trump’s talks with Cuba will not lead to wholesale political transformation.
item.dc:creator: Patricia Mazzei
item.media:credit: Marco Bello/Reuters
item.media:description: Supporters of President Trump protesting Cuba’s government in Miami in February.

item.title: Bipartisan Bill Would Waive $100,000 H-1B Visa Fees for Doctors and Nurses
item.description: The bill, sponsored by two Republicans and two Democrats in the House, seeks to alleviate shortages of doctors and nurses in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Roni Caryn Rabin
item.media:credit: Morgan Lieberman for The New York Times
item.media:description: U.S. teaching hospitals rely heavily on newly minted foreign medical school graduates.

item.title: Israel, Generational Divide Animate Democratic House Primaries in Illinois
item.description: Crowded Democratic fields will determine the power of pro-Israel lobbying dollars and how eager voters are for a new crop of leaders.
item.dc:creator: Kellen Browning
item.media:credit: Jim Vondruska/Reuters
item.media:description: Kat Abughazaleh, a 26-year-old progressive content creator and a former researcher for a left-wing media organization, represents a wave of younger and nontraditional candidates seeking to displace Democratic stalwarts in 2026.

item.title: Ex-L.A. County Deputy Sentenced to 5 Years in Cryptocurrency Extortion Plot
item.description: The former deputy with the Los Angeles County Sheriff’s Department pleaded guilty last year to conspiracy charges for his role in the scheme, according to federal prosecutors.
item.dc:creator: Aimee Ortiz
item.media:credit: Google Maps
item.media:description: The headquarters for the Los Angeles County Sheriff’s Department.

item.title: Chief Justice Says Personal Attacks on Judges Are ‘Dangerous’ and Must Stop
item.description: The public remarks from Chief Justice John Roberts were his first since President Trump excoriated the justices who ruled against his tariffs in harsh and personal terms.
item.dc:creator: Ann E. Marimow
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
item.media:description: Chief Justice John G. Roberts Jr. during the State of the Union address last month. He has previously objected to attacks on judges, but primarily in writing.

item.title: Illinois voters are choosing a successor to Senator Richard J. Durbin, long a force on Capitol Hill.
item.description: Senator Richard J. Durbin, the No. 2 Senate Democrat, has been a leading liberal voice during his decades in office.
item.dc:creator: Carl Hulse
item.category: Dream Act (Development, Relief and Education for Minors Act)
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Senator Richard J. Durbin, Democrat of Illinois, has served in the Senate since 1997.

item.title: Trump Says U.S. Doesn’t Need Allies to Reopen Strait of Hormuz
item.description: President Trump has faced mounting criticism over the U.S.-Israeli assault on Iran from allies and even members of his own administration.
item.dc:creator: Karoun Demirjian and David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump spoke to reporters on Tuesday while meeting with Micheál Martin, the prime minister of Ireland, in the Oval Office.

item.title: Raja Krishnamoorthi Has a Huge Ad Spending Advantage in Illinois Senate Race
item.description: More than two-thirds of the media buys in the Illinois Senate race have gone toward Representative Raja Krishnamoorthi, with much of that coming from his own campaign.
item.dc:creator: Matt Zdun
item.media:credit: Joshua Lott for The New York Times
item.media:description: Representative Raja Krishnamoorthi, Democrat of Illinois, raised more than $30 million for his campaign for a U.S. Senate seat.

item.title: Illinois Primaries Flooded With Money From AIPAC and Cryptocurrency
item.description: Various groups connected to the pro-Israel lobby have spent millions on House races, while cryptocurrency super PACs have spent heavily in the Democratic primary for Senate.
item.dc:creator: Matt Zdun
item.media:credit: Jim Vondruska for The New York Times
item.media:description: Super PACs connected to various groups have spent tens of millions of dollars in advertisements for Tuesday’s primary elections in Illinois.

item.title: Trump Says Newsom Shouldn’t Be President Because He Is Dyslexic
item.description: President Trump said “a president should not have learning disabilities,” prompting criticism from a group that advocates equal opportunities for people with learning disabilities.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk
item.media:credit: Sean Rayford/Getty Images
item.media:description: Gov. Gavin Newsom of California addressing the crowd at an event hosted by the South Carolina Democratic Party in February.

item.title: A Meteor Streaks Across the U.S. and Rattles Ohio With an Explosive Boom
item.description: NASA confirmed that the loud boom heard and felt by people in northern Ohio was indeed caused by a meteor.
item.dc:creator: Christine Hauser

item.title: A St. Patrick’s Day Election in Chicago? Politicians Look for Luck in the Timing.
item.description: In a county with the largest Irish American population in the U.S., a crush of parades has been a bounty for candidates searching for votes.
item.dc:creator: Julie Bosman
item.media:credit: Jamie Kelter Davis for The New York Times
item.media:description: Raja Krishnamoorthi, a candidate for the U.S. Senate, marched with other Illinois politicians in a St. Patrick’s Day parade in Chicago.

item.title: Trump’s Gutting of Election Security Fuels Worries for Midterms
item.description: Officials say the crippling of the Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency, which the president himself created, could open elections to cyberattacks and foreign influence.
item.dc:creator: Adam Sella
item.media:credit: Zaydee Sanchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency was created to protect critical infrastructure, including elections, from cyberthreats.

item.title: What’s in Trump’s SAVE Act, the Voter ID Bill Republicans Are Pushing?
item.description: The legislation would require voters to prove their citizenship in person upon registration, ban IDs without a photo at polling places and criminalize failures to enforce such requirements.
item.dc:creator: Minho Kim
item.media:credit: Hiroko Masuike/The New York Times
item.media:description: A polling station in New York City during a mayoral election last year.

item.title: Joe Kent, a Top Counterterrorism Official for the Trump Administration, Resigns, Citing Iran War
item.description: Mr. Kent is the highest-ranking Trump administration official to quit in opposition to the conflict. He is also known for his support of conspiracy theories.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes, Robert Draper and Luke Broadwater
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Jenny Kane/Associated Press
item.media:description: “I cannot in good conscience support the ongoing war in Iran,” Joe Kent, the director of the National Counterterrorism Center, wrote in a letter to President Trump that he posted Tuesday.

item.title: Jesse Jackson’s Son Disputes Claim That His Father Endorsed Stratton in Senate Race
item.description: Days after Lt. Gov. Juliana Stratton said the late civil rights leader had supported her campaign, one of his sons said the elder Mr. Jackson had not made a final choice.
item.dc:creator: Reid J. Epstein
item.media:credit: Jamie Kelter Davis for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Rev. Jesse Jackson in July. He died last month.

item.title: AIPAC, the Pro-Israel Group, Becomes a Major Issue in Illinois Democratic House Races
item.description: Several groups with ties to the pro-Israel group have spent at least $20 million in four primaries backing candidates who are likely to support their cause in Washington.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Medina
item.media:credit: Photographs by Joshua Lott for The New York Times
item.media:description: Groups associated with the American Israel Public Affairs Committee have focused much of their spending in the Ninth District, seeking to bolster Laura Fine, a state senator, against Daniel Biss, the mayor of Evanston.

item.title: When does your state vote? Here’s the 2026 primary calendar.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.media:credit: The New York Times

item.title: The Illinois primary shows the rising political influence of Indian Americans.
item.dc:creator: Reid J. Epstein
item.media:credit: Joshua Lott for The New York Times
item.media:description: Representative Raja Krishnamoorthi is a Democratic candidate for Senate.

item.title: A House candidate is attacking an ad praising her.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk
item.media:credit: Nam Y. Huh/Associated Press
item.media:description: Bushra Amiwala, a Democratic candidate for Congress, in Chicago last month.
